name: sled hack squat
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable position for your height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Hold onto the handles or bars for stability.
Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to raise your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable position for your height. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards. Hold onto the handles or bars for stability. Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and chest up. Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below. Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to raise your body back up to the starting position. conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
